message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="heydon-pickering" color="red" summary="+1 Scott's review, name normalization helps a11y too">
+1 to Scott's domain review. No additional a11y concerns to raise beyond what I have already stated.

One small connection: Scott's observation about `ContextName::parse()` normalizing the `@` prefix is relevant from a user-facing perspective too. Consistent naming means screen reader users will hear consistent context names across the UI (e.g., always "@computer", never sometimes "computer" and sometimes "@computer"). The parse-don't-validate pattern serves accessibility indirectly by preventing inconsistent data from reaching the templates.

Otherwise, I agree with the emerging consensus: good quality, process improvement needed on commit granularity.
</teammate-message>

content.text:
Good observation from Heydon — the parse-don't-validate pattern serving accessibility indirectly through data consistency is a nice insight. The retro is already closed with 9/9 consensus, so this is noted for the record but doesn't change our action items.

Standing by for the next task from the coordinator.
